query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "audio interaction model" computer science
rationale: This paper belongs in the literature review because it directly addresses the "Audio Interaction Model" domain by proposing a specific architecture (DeSTA2.5-Audio) and methodology (self-generated cross-modal alignment) for Large Audio Language Models, satisfying criteria (a) for same-mechanism evidence and (d) as a foundational reference for current LALM development strategies.
